taskDescription: Arrange the steps of examining the upper limb in correct anatomical sequence (superficial to deep).
paragraphs: Identify superficial fascia containing fat, cutaneous nerves, and superficial veins (cephalic and basilic veins).
Examine the deep fascia wrapping around the muscles and forming the intermuscular septa that divide the arm into compartments.
Locate the anterior compartment containing the flexor muscles (biceps brachii, brachialis, coracobrachialis).
Identify the posterior compartment containing the extensor muscle (triceps brachii).
Examine the deeper structures: joints (elbow, wrist, carpometacarpal) with their articular surfaces, ligaments, and neurovascular supply.
